page: Amazon Web Services inf2.48xlarge is an Inferentia Gen2 instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 virtual CPUs mapped to 96 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at a frequency of 3.6 GHz. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is not included on this instance, but it features a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the inf2.48xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, scoring in the top 10% on stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU tests, including physics, encryption, and extended instructions. Single-core performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests. Memory bandwidth for cached small blocks is exceptionally high, placing in the top 10%, while uncached large-block read and write bandwidths remain strong in the top 25%. However, the PassMark memory score is relatively low, falling in the bottom 10%. For database operations, the instance achieves top-tier performance in Redis SET operations, processing 172.89 million operations per second, while standard PassMark database operations score in the average tier.

Given its high vCPU density and substantial memory capacity, the inf2.48xlarge is well-suited for compute-heavy workloads, multi-threaded applications, and high-throughput network services. It achieves strong performance in software compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving. For language model processing, the instance delivers strong prompt processing speeds for small and medium models, though text generation speeds fall into the average range. The absence of local storage means that storage-intensive database workloads will rely entirely on network-attached storage, making this instance most cost-efficient for compute-bound tasks, distributed microservices, and high-throughput API endpoints that leverage its 100 Gbps network interface.
description: Amazon Web Services inf2.48xlarge is an Inferentia Gen2 instance featuring 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a 100 Gbps baseline network interface, with no local storage. The instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% on stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests, alongside strong cached memory bandwidth and high-throughput Redis SET operations. However, its PassMark memory score is in the bottom 10%. The inf2.48xlarge is highly effective for multi-threaded compute tasks, software compilation, ray tracing, and high-bandwidth network services, though storage-dependent workloads must rely on network-attached volumes.
og_description: Amazon Web Services inf2.48xlarge is a Nitro-based instance featuring 192 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and Redis performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services inf2.48xlarge features 192 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for high-compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring 192 vCPUs, 100 Gbps networking, and top-tier multi-core performance for demanding parallel workloads.

768.0 GB of DDR4 memory with 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and high-throughput Redis operations
No local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions
